thesis_status_reasoning: The architecture shows surface stability (AFN at par, ASE appreciating, deposits growing) but critical structural fragility is evident. The Stabilization Consortium coverage ratio of 17.8% is below the 20% WARNING threshold and approaching the 15% TRIGGERED level, while the High-Yield Treasury Facility concentration ratio of 78.4% exceeds the 75% structural fragility threshold. Most concerning: the posted yield of 24.2% versus estimated organic yield of 8.1% represents a 16.1 percentage point gap (199% of organic yield), far exceeding the 10% WARNING threshold for yield sustainability. This configuration indicates the system is operating on subsidy, not organic economics.
action_reasoning: Two kill switches are currently in WARNING status (Reserve Coverage, Facility Yield Sustainability) and one has triggered (Deposit Concentration > 75%). While no CRITICAL conditions exist yet, the thesis is structurally pre-loaded. The compound stress matrix indicates reduced resilience: less external stress is now required to reach critical thresholds because multiple legs are already elevated.

key_signals.signal: Reserve Coverage Ratio at 17.8% — below 20% WARNING threshold, only 2.8 percentage points above 15% TRIGGERED level
Yield gap of 16.1 percentage points (24.2% posted vs 8.1% organic) — system requires 199% subsidy over organic yield to maintain deposits
Deposit concentration at 78.4% exceeds 75% structural fragility threshold — High-Yield Treasury Facility holds more than three-quarters of all outstanding AFN
AFN par stability at $100.02 (0.02% deviation) with zero swap mechanism activation — debt-to-equity mechanism remains untested under stress
ASE price appreciation of 3.6% with $3.64B daily volume — equity instrument showing strength and liquidity
Net deposits of $585M in period with only $78M redemptions — deposit growth continues but redemption pressure is minimal, mechanism untested

biggest_uncertainty: Whether the 16.1 percentage point yield subsidy is a temporary promotional strategy or a permanent structural requirement — if the latter, the architecture cannot sustain institutional deposits without continuous foundation subsidy, making it fundamentally non-viable as a sovereign debt instrument
